schema_version.description: Payload schema_version. 1.0.0 = legacy ungrounded; 1.1.0 = Phase Y grounded (PR #128 source_turns contract — unchanged here); 1.2.0 = additionally permits the optional rationale (decisions) / claims with external_references + evidence_in_transcript / follow_up_required (action_items) / word_level_timestamps / sentiment_indicators / meeting_phases fields; 1.3.0 = additionally permits eight new optional cross-meeting arrays (issue_registry_entry, position_statement, dissent_or_objection, agenda_item, precedent_reference, external_stakeholder_input, glossary_definition, procedural_ruling) plus the optional claim_complexity field on claims items; 1.4.0 = Phase 1 verbatim span grounding: every item-type sub-schema declares a `grounding_mode` discriminator and adds the optional grounding fields (verbatim items add source_quote / quote_offset_normalized / quote_offset_original, turn_aggregate items add source_turn_ids). The new fields are OPTIONAL at the JSON-Schema layer so pre-1.4 artifacts validate unchanged; the promotion gate (`promotion/gate.py`) is what enforces that 1.4.0 artifacts carry them. Additivity: every 1.2.0 / 1.3.0 / 1.4.0 field is optional in the schema, so legacy artifacts validate unchanged and an artifact may declare 1.4.0 without carrying any new field.; 1.5.0 = Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM: every claim-shaped item adds the optional source_chunk_id field for chunk-scoped grounding, and the new src/spectrum_systems_core/promotion/grounding_gate.py module enforces that source_quote (when present) is a literal substring of the named chunk (or the full transcript when source_chunk_id is omitted) after normalization (whitespace, smart quotes, NFKC). source_quote and source_chunk_id remain OPTIONAL at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy artifacts validate unchanged; the 1.5.0 gate enforces presence and substring-match for the 14 claim-shaped types.; 1.6.0 = Phase 4.B G-ACTION-ITEMS-DICT: action_items items are now object-only (the bare-string branch from 1.0.0-1.5.0 is removed). A bare string cannot carry a `source_quote` so the Phase 4.A grounding gate already blocks bare action_items as `is a bare value, cannot carry source_quote`; tightening the schema fail-closed delivers the same enforcement at the structural layer so producers cannot regress. This is a BREAKING change for any legacy producer that emitted bare-string action_items — the regex `meeting_minutes.py` workflow and any test fixture both must emit objects. open_questions and the bare-string form of decisions are unchanged.

decisions.description: Additive (schema-version-agnostic): an item may be a legacy verbatim decision STRING or a structured object. The string branch keeps every existing regex / LLM artifact and the legacy LLM strict-schema path valid. The object branch carries the canonical typed-decision shape (`text`, optional `verb`) plus the two optional architecture-review fields `stakeholders` and `confidence`. `text`/`verb` match what evals/regulatory_verb.py reads so an object-form decision is still verb-classified, not silently bypassed.

stakeholders.description: Optional, default []. Names of stakeholders affected by or responsible for this decision. Empty array when the transcript does not make it clear (default applied by the producer/model, not enforced by JSON Schema).

confidence.description: Optional. Model confidence 0.0-1.0 that this is a real decision vs. discussion. Null or omitted when uncertain; any number outside [0.0, 1.0] fails the gate.

rationale.description: Optional (schema_version 1.2.0+). The stated reason or justification WHY this decision was made, as expressed in the transcript — the explicit 'why', distinct from background decision context. Null or omitted when no rationale was stated; legacy 1.0.0 / 1.1.0 decisions omit it entirely (additivity).

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: decisions are a VERBATIM item type. Optional at the JSON-Schema layer for additivity (legacy 1.0.0 / 1.1.0 / 1.2.0 / 1.3.0 artifacts omit it). The promotion gate (`promotion/gate.py`) enforces that 1.4.0 artifacts carry it together with source_quote / quote_offset_normalized / quote_offset_original.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). The verbatim substring of the transcript that supports this decision. Copy character-for-character including speech errors. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the promotion gate enforces presence and byte-match.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset of source_quote against the normalized transcript. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence and verifies the byte-match at this offset.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the ORIGINAL transcript. Computed by the gate from quote_offset_normalized via the normalization position map; producers may emit a best estimate, the gate re-derives the authoritative value.

reason.description: Phase 3.A (additive, optional; superseded Phase 3P, retained for parity with the other 13 reason-bearing types). One-sentence explanation of WHY this item was extracted — the trigger phrase or group affirmation. Required by the prompt; optional in JSON Schema for backward-compat. A high missing-rate across an extraction is recorded as the `few_shot_reason_missing_rate` diagnostic on the per-run `pipeline_invocation_log`.

decision_subtype.description: Phase 3.B (additive, optional). Implicit-decision sub-type from the Fernández et al. (SIGDIAL 2008) taxonomy as adapted in workflows/prompts/meeting_minutes_llm.md and meeting_minutes_opus.md: `issue` (problem identification), `proposal` (course of action proposed), `resolution` (group commits without formal vote), `scope` (boundary ruling). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer for additivity (pre-3.B artifacts omit it). The enum is the closed taxonomy; any other string fails the gate.

source_chunk_id.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). Optional chunk identifier the source_quote was lifted from. When present, the Phase 4.A substring grounding gate (`promotion/grounding_gate.py`) scopes its check to this chunk only. When absent, the gate falls back to checking against the full transcript text (weaker — and logged as a warning in the grounding_gate_result artifact). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy 1.0.0-1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. Speaker turn_ids (as strings) supporting this decision. Mirrors the `source_turns` field on `issue_registry_entry` / `position_statement`. Distinct from the Phase 1 / Phase 4.A verbatim grounding contract (`source_quote` + `source_chunk_id`), which the gate enforces independently. Optional in schema so legacy artifacts validate unchanged.

action_items.description: Phase 4.B (schema_version 1.6.0): items MUST be a structured object. The bare-string branch from earlier schema versions is removed because a bare string cannot carry a `source_quote` and the Phase 4.A grounding gate rejects it as `is a bare value, cannot carry source_quote`. Producers must always emit the object form so the gate can verify the verbatim anchor.

status.description: Optional. Default "open" when absent (default applied by the producer, not enforced by JSON Schema). Only these three values are valid — e.g. "done" fails the gate.

follow_up_required.description: Optional (schema_version 1.2.0+). Default true (applied by the producer, not enforced by JSON Schema): true when a human must take action before the next meeting, false for completed or informational items. Legacy action_item objects omit it (additivity).

priority.description: Optional. Derived from the modal verb policy in workflows/prompts/meeting_minutes_llm.md (PR #190): 'shall'/'will' → high, 'should' → medium, 'may' → low. Omit the key entirely when no modal-verb signal is present (additivity: legacy action_item objects validate unchanged). Only these three values are valid; any other (e.g. "urgent") fails the gate, and null is rejected.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: action_items are a VERBATIM item type. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the gate enforces presence and byte-match.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Verbatim transcript substring supporting this action_item.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the normalized transcript.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the original transcript (re-derived by the gate).

reason.description: Phase 3.A (additive, optional; superseded Phase 3P, retained for parity with the other 13 reason-bearing types). One-sentence explanation of WHY this action_item was extracted — the trigger phrase, modal verb, or assignment that made it qualify. Required by the prompt; optional in JSON Schema for backward-compat.

source_chunk_id.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). Optional chunk identifier the source_quote was lifted from. When present, the Phase 4.A substring grounding gate (`promotion/grounding_gate.py`) scopes its check to this chunk only. When absent, the gate falls back to checking against the full transcript text (weaker — and logged as a warning in the grounding_gate_result artifact). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy 1.0.0-1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. Speaker turn_ids (as strings) supporting this action_item. Mirrors the `source_turns` field on `issue_registry_entry` / `position_statement`. Distinct from the Phase 1 / Phase 4.A verbatim grounding contract (`source_quote` + `source_chunk_id`), which the gate enforces independently. Optional in schema so legacy artifacts validate unchanged.

open_questions.description: Additive: items may be a legacy string OR a structured Q&A-log object. The string branch preserves existing artifacts and the LLM strict-schema eval.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: open_questions aggregate across turns (Q&A flow). Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence together with source_turn_ids for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_turn_ids.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Turn IDs this Q&A spans. Optional in schema; the gate enforces every id exists in the transcript turn index.

reason.description: Phase 3.A (additive, optional). Why the speaker is making this claim/decision/action/etc. Must be derivable from the source span. Required by the prompt; optional in JSON Schema for backward-compat with pre-3.A artifacts.

source_chunk_id.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). Optional chunk identifier the source_quote was lifted from. When present, the Phase 4.A substring grounding gate (`promotion/grounding_gate.py`) scopes its check to this chunk only. When absent, the gate falls back to checking against the full transcript text (weaker — and logged as a warning in the grounding_gate_result artifact). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy 1.0.0-1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

source_quote.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). The verbatim transcript substring that supports this item. Reproduce transcription errors AS-IS. The Phase 4.A substring grounding gate enforces a normalized length >= 10 chars and a literal substring match against the chunk named by source_chunk_id (or against the full transcript when source_chunk_id is absent). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy artifacts validate.

commitments.description: Optional, default []. Individual "I will do X" statements distinct from group action_items.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type is VERBATIM and carries a source_quote anchored by byte offset. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the gate enforces presence + byte-match for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Verbatim transcript substring supporting this item. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset of source_quote against the normalized transcript. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence and byte-match at this offset.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the original transcript (re-derived by the gate from the normalization position map).

reason.description: Phase 3.A (additive, optional). Why the speaker is making this claim/decision/action/etc. Must be derivable from the source span. Required by the prompt; optional in JSON Schema for backward-compat with pre-3.A artifacts.

source_chunk_id.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). Optional chunk identifier the source_quote was lifted from. When present, the Phase 4.A substring grounding gate (`promotion/grounding_gate.py`) scopes its check to this chunk only. When absent, the gate falls back to checking against the full transcript text (weaker — and logged as a warning in the grounding_gate_result artifact). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy 1.0.0-1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. Speaker turn_ids (as strings) supporting this commitment. Mirrors the `source_turns` field on `issue_registry_entry` / `position_statement`. Distinct from the Phase 1 / Phase 4.A verbatim grounding contract (`source_quote` + `source_chunk_id`), which the gate enforces independently. Optional in schema so legacy artifacts validate unchanged.

risks.description: Optional, default []. Things a speaker flagged as a potential problem.

severity.description: Optional severity. Null when the transcript does not state one. Any value outside low/medium/high (and null) fails the gate.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type is VERBATIM and carries a source_quote anchored by byte offset. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the gate enforces presence + byte-match for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Verbatim transcript substring supporting this item. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset of source_quote against the normalized transcript. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence and byte-match at this offset.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the original transcript (re-derived by the gate from the normalization position map).

reason.description: Phase 3.A (additive, optional). Why the speaker is making this claim/decision/action/etc. Must be derivable from the source span. Required by the prompt; optional in JSON Schema for backward-compat with pre-3.A artifacts.

source_chunk_id.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). Optional chunk identifier the source_quote was lifted from. When present, the Phase 4.A substring grounding gate (`promotion/grounding_gate.py`) scopes its check to this chunk only. When absent, the gate falls back to checking against the full transcript text (weaker — and logged as a warning in the grounding_gate_result artifact). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy 1.0.0-1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. Speaker turn_ids (as strings) supporting this risk. Mirrors the `source_turns` field on `issue_registry_entry` / `position_statement`. Distinct from the Phase 1 / Phase 4.A verbatim grounding contract (`source_quote` + `source_chunk_id`), which the gate enforces independently. Optional in schema so legacy artifacts validate unchanged.

claims.description: Optional, default []. Factual or analytical assertions made in the meeting, distinct from `decisions` (which commit the group) and `risks` (flagged problems). Legacy 1.0.0 / 1.1.0 artifacts omit this array entirely (additivity).

external_references.description: Optional (schema_version 1.2.0+), default [] (applied by the producer/model, not enforced by JSON Schema). External documents, ITU articles, CFR sections, or other sources cited as evidence for this claim. E.g. ["ITU Article 21", "47 CFR 96.41", "OB3 Section 4.2"]. Empty array when none cited.

evidence_in_transcript.description: Optional (schema_version 1.2.0+), default [] (applied by the producer/model). turn_ids that provide evidence SUPPORTING this claim — deliberately distinct from the PR #128 grounding/source_turns contract, which records where the claim was STATED. A claim may be stated in one turn but supported by evidence across multiple turns. This field does NOT alter or re-pin the grounding contract.

claim_complexity.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+). atomic = single independently verifiable fact; compound = bundles multiple facts that should be split. Default "atomic" (applied by the producer/model, not enforced by JSON Schema): the key is OPTIONAL so every legacy 1.0.0 / 1.1.0 / 1.2.0 claim — and any claim that simply omits it — validates unchanged. Only these two values are valid; any other (e.g. "wrong") fails the gate.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type is VERBATIM and carries a source_quote anchored by byte offset. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the gate enforces presence + byte-match for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Verbatim transcript substring supporting this item. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset of source_quote against the normalized transcript. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence and byte-match at this offset.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the original transcript (re-derived by the gate from the normalization position map).

reason.description: Phase 3.A (additive, optional). Why the speaker is making this claim/decision/action/etc. Must be derivable from the source span. Required by the prompt; optional in JSON Schema for backward-compat with pre-3.A artifacts.

source_chunk_id.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). Optional chunk identifier the source_quote was lifted from. When present, the Phase 4.A substring grounding gate (`promotion/grounding_gate.py`) scopes its check to this chunk only. When absent, the gate falls back to checking against the full transcript text (weaker — and logged as a warning in the grounding_gate_result artifact). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy 1.0.0-1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. Speaker turn_ids (as strings) supporting this claim. Mirrors the `source_turns` field on `issue_registry_entry` / `position_statement`. Distinct from `evidence_in_transcript` (turns providing supporting evidence, schema_version 1.2.0+) and from the Phase 1 / Phase 4.A verbatim grounding contract (`source_quote` + `source_chunk_id`), which the gate enforces independently. Optional in schema so legacy artifacts validate unchanged.

cross_references.description: Optional, default []. References to other meetings or documents.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type AGGREGATES across multiple transcript turns rather than pointing to one quote. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence + every source_turn_ids entry exists in the transcript turn index.

source_turn_ids.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Turn IDs this aggregate spans. Optional in schema; the gate enforces every id exists in the transcript turn index for 1.4.0 artifacts.

reason.description: Phase 3.A (additive, optional). Why the speaker is making this claim/decision/action/etc. Must be derivable from the source span. Required by the prompt; optional in JSON Schema for backward-compat with pre-3.A artifacts.

source_chunk_id.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). Optional chunk identifier the source_quote was lifted from. When present, the Phase 4.A substring grounding gate (`promotion/grounding_gate.py`) scopes its check to this chunk only. When absent, the gate falls back to checking against the full transcript text (weaker — and logged as a warning in the grounding_gate_result artifact). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy 1.0.0-1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

source_quote.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). The verbatim transcript substring that supports this item. Reproduce transcription errors AS-IS. The Phase 4.A substring grounding gate enforces a normalized length >= 10 chars and a literal substring match against the chunk named by source_chunk_id (or against the full transcript when source_chunk_id is absent). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy artifacts validate.

attendees.description: Optional, default []. Roster with name, agency, role.

agency.description: The attendee's agency as stated in the transcript. The key is required (the extraction model always emits it) but the value is null when the transcript names a participant without stating their agency — a named attendee with no stated agency is still a real, faithful extraction (the model must never invent an agency), so agency: null is valid and must not block. minLength still applies to the string branch so an empty-string agency stays rejected; a non-string/non-null value is still rejected fail-closed.

present.description: Optional. Default true when absent (default applied by the producer).

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type AGGREGATES across multiple transcript turns rather than pointing to one quote. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence + every source_turn_ids entry exists in the transcript turn index.

source_turn_ids.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Turn IDs this aggregate spans. Optional in schema; the gate enforces every id exists in the transcript turn index for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. Speaker turn_ids (as strings) supporting this entry. Mirrors the `source_turns` field on `issue_registry_entry` / `position_statement` / `dissent_or_objection`. Distinct from the integer-typed `source_turn_ids` (Phase 1 grounding contract). Optional in schema so legacy artifacts validate unchanged.

start_turn.description: Optional (additive). First speaker turn where this topic appears. Mirrors the existing `start_timestamp` and complements `meeting_phases.start_turn_id` / `agenda_item.start_turn_id`. Null or omitted when not stated.

end_turn.description: Optional (additive). Last speaker turn where this topic appears. Mirrors the existing `end_timestamp` and complements `meeting_phases.end_turn_id` / `agenda_item.end_turn_id`. Null or omitted when not stated.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type AGGREGATES across multiple transcript turns rather than pointing to one quote. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence + every source_turn_ids entry exists in the transcript turn index.

source_turn_ids.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Turn IDs this aggregate spans. Optional in schema; the gate enforces every id exists in the transcript turn index for 1.4.0 artifacts.

regulatory_references.description: Optional, default []. OB3, statutory citations, rule references.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type is VERBATIM and carries a source_quote anchored by byte offset. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the gate enforces presence + byte-match for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Verbatim transcript substring supporting this item. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset of source_quote against the normalized transcript. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence and byte-match at this offset.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the original transcript (re-derived by the gate from the normalization position map).

reason.description: Phase 3.A (additive, optional). Why the speaker is making this claim/decision/action/etc. Must be derivable from the source span. Required by the prompt; optional in JSON Schema for backward-compat with pre-3.A artifacts.

source_chunk_id.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). Optional chunk identifier the source_quote was lifted from. When present, the Phase 4.A substring grounding gate (`promotion/grounding_gate.py`) scopes its check to this chunk only. When absent, the gate falls back to checking against the full transcript text (weaker — and logged as a warning in the grounding_gate_result artifact). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy 1.0.0-1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

technical_parameters.description: Optional, default []. Exact numeric values (frequencies, bands, thresholds) stated verbatim.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type is VERBATIM and carries a source_quote anchored by byte offset. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the gate enforces presence + byte-match for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Verbatim transcript substring supporting this item. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset of source_quote against the normalized transcript. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence and byte-match at this offset.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the original transcript (re-derived by the gate from the normalization position map).

named_artifacts.description: Optional, default []. Documents, folders, reports mentioned by name.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type AGGREGATES across multiple transcript turns rather than pointing to one quote. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence + every source_turn_ids entry exists in the transcript turn index.

source_turn_ids.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Turn IDs this aggregate spans. Optional in schema; the gate enforces every id exists in the transcript turn index for 1.4.0 artifacts.

scheduled_events.description: Optional, default []. Future meetings/events with dates and purposes.

date.description: The event date as stated in the transcript. The key is required (the extraction model always emits it) but the value is null when no date was explicitly stated — a scheduled event mentioned without a date is still a real, faithful extraction, so null is valid and must not block (the model must never invent a date).

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. Speaker turn_ids (as strings) supporting this entry. Mirrors the `source_turns` field on `issue_registry_entry` / `position_statement`. Distinct from the integer-typed `source_turn_ids` (Phase 1 grounding contract). Optional in schema so legacy artifacts validate unchanged.

start_turn.description: Optional (additive). First speaker turn where this scheduled event was discussed. Null or omitted when not stated.

end_turn.description: Optional (additive). Last speaker turn where this scheduled event was discussed. Null or omitted when not stated.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type AGGREGATES across multiple transcript turns rather than pointing to one quote. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence + every source_turn_ids entry exists in the transcript turn index.

source_turn_ids.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Turn IDs this aggregate spans. Optional in schema; the gate enforces every id exists in the transcript turn index for 1.4.0 artifacts.

word_level_timestamps.description: Optional (schema_version 1.2.0+), default false. Whether the chunks.jsonl for this transcript carries word-level timestamps. Set by the chunker at ingestion time, NEVER by the extraction model. False for the current docx inputs (no word-level timing data); infrastructure for future diarized transcripts. Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

sentiment_indicators.description: Optional (schema_version 1.2.0+), default []. Speaker turns flagged for notable sentiment. Only populated when a speaker expresses clear disagreement, concern, strong endorsement, uncertainty, or frustration — not routine discussion. Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

sentiment.description: Exactly one of the five approved sentiment signals. Any other value (e.g. "annoyed") fails the gate.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type is VERBATIM and carries a source_quote anchored by byte offset. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the gate enforces presence + byte-match for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Verbatim transcript substring supporting this item. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset of source_quote against the normalized transcript. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence and byte-match at this offset.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the original transcript (re-derived by the gate from the normalization position map).

meeting_phases.description: Optional (schema_version 1.2.0+), default []. High-level phases of the meeting in sequence. Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

phase_name.description: Exactly one of the five approved phase names. Any other value (e.g. "lunch") fails the gate.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. Speaker turn_ids (as strings) supporting this entry. Mirrors the `source_turns` field on `issue_registry_entry` / `position_statement`. Distinct from the integer-typed `source_turn_ids` (Phase 1 grounding contract). Optional in schema so legacy artifacts validate unchanged.

start_turn.description: Optional (additive). First speaker turn where this phase begins. Mirrors the existing `start_turn_id` field. Null or omitted when not stated.

end_turn.description: Optional (additive). Last speaker turn where this phase ends. Mirrors the existing `end_turn_id` field. Null or omitted when not stated.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type AGGREGATES across multiple transcript turns rather than pointing to one quote. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence + every source_turn_ids entry exists in the transcript turn index.

source_turn_ids.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Turn IDs this aggregate spans. Optional in schema; the gate enforces every id exists in the transcript turn index for 1.4.0 artifacts.

issue_registry_entry.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. Substantive technical or policy problems being worked across multiple meetings; an issue persists until resolved. DISTINCT from `open_questions` (a procedural question raised in one meeting) and `decisions` (a resolution). Legacy 1.0.0 / 1.1.0 / 1.2.0 artifacts omit it entirely (additivity — the array is optional, so absence validates).

issue_type.description: Exactly one of the five approved issue types. Any other value fails the gate.

status.description: Exactly one of the four approved statuses. Any other value (e.g. "done") fails the gate.

related_decisions.description: Optional, default []. Decision text or ids this issue is related to.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. turn_ids supporting this entry.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type is VERBATIM and carries a source_quote anchored by byte offset. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the gate enforces presence + byte-match for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Verbatim transcript substring supporting this item. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset of source_quote against the normalized transcript. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence and byte-match at this offset.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the original transcript (re-derived by the gate from the normalization position map).

reason.description: Phase 3.A (additive, optional). Why the speaker is making this claim/decision/action/etc. Must be derivable from the source span. Required by the prompt; optional in JSON Schema for backward-compat with pre-3.A artifacts.

source_chunk_id.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). Optional chunk identifier the source_quote was lifted from. When present, the Phase 4.A substring grounding gate (`promotion/grounding_gate.py`) scopes its check to this chunk only. When absent, the gate falls back to checking against the full transcript text (weaker — and logged as a warning in the grounding_gate_result artifact). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy 1.0.0-1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

position_statement.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. An agency's or participant's stated stance on a topic that may recur or evolve across meetings — a declared position, NOT a decision (a stance, not a resolution). Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

position_type.description: Exactly one of the six approved position types. `clarification` covers a participant clarifying their agency's stance without taking a clear support/opposition position (added additively; pre-existing artifacts validate unchanged). Any other value fails the gate.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. turn_ids supporting this statement.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type is VERBATIM and carries a source_quote anchored by byte offset. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the gate enforces presence + byte-match for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Verbatim transcript substring supporting this item. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset of source_quote against the normalized transcript. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence and byte-match at this offset.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the original transcript (re-derived by the gate from the normalization position map).

reason.description: Phase 3.A (additive, optional). Why the speaker is making this claim/decision/action/etc. Must be derivable from the source span. Required by the prompt; optional in JSON Schema for backward-compat with pre-3.A artifacts.

source_chunk_id.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). Optional chunk identifier the source_quote was lifted from. When present, the Phase 4.A substring grounding gate (`promotion/grounding_gate.py`) scopes its check to this chunk only. When absent, the gate falls back to checking against the full transcript text (weaker — and logged as a warning in the grounding_gate_result artifact). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy 1.0.0-1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

dissent_or_objection.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. A formal disagreement explicitly registered on the record by a participant. DISTINCT from `risks` (a flagged potential problem) and `sentiment_indicators` (tone). Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

resolved.description: Optional. Default false (applied by the producer/model, not enforced by JSON Schema).

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. turn_ids supporting this objection.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type is VERBATIM and carries a source_quote anchored by byte offset. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the gate enforces presence + byte-match for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Verbatim transcript substring supporting this item. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset of source_quote against the normalized transcript. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence and byte-match at this offset.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the original transcript (re-derived by the gate from the normalization position map).

reason.description: Phase 3.A (additive, optional). Why the speaker is making this claim/decision/action/etc. Must be derivable from the source span. Required by the prompt; optional in JSON Schema for backward-compat with pre-3.A artifacts.

source_chunk_id.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). Optional chunk identifier the source_quote was lifted from. When present, the Phase 4.A substring grounding gate (`promotion/grounding_gate.py`) scopes its check to this chunk only. When absent, the gate falls back to checking against the full transcript text (weaker — and logged as a warning in the grounding_gate_result artifact). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy 1.0.0-1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

agenda_item.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. Formal agenda structure recoverable from the transcript (numbered or titled sections) — the organizational spine for cross-meeting document structure, NOT the substantive content (that stays in `topics` / `decisions`). Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

summary.description: Optional (additive). Brief description of what this agenda item covers. Mirrors the existing `summary` field on `topics` and `meeting_phases`. Optional in schema so legacy 1.3.0/1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. Speaker turn_ids (as strings) supporting this entry. Mirrors the `source_turns` field on `issue_registry_entry` / `position_statement`. Distinct from the integer-typed `source_turn_ids` (Phase 1 grounding contract). Optional in schema so legacy artifacts validate unchanged.

start_turn.description: Optional (additive). First speaker turn where this agenda item begins. Mirrors the existing `start_turn_id` field. Null or omitted when not stated.

end_turn.description: Optional (additive). Last speaker turn where this agenda item ends. Mirrors the existing `end_turn_id` field. Null or omitted when not stated.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type AGGREGATES across multiple transcript turns rather than pointing to one quote. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence + every source_turn_ids entry exists in the transcript turn index.

source_turn_ids.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Turn IDs this aggregate spans. Optional in schema; the gate enforces every id exists in the transcript turn index for 1.4.0 artifacts.

precedent_reference.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. A speaker's reference to a prior meeting, prior decision, or prior study used as justification for a current position or direction. At scale these form a citation graph across the TIG process. Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

purpose.description: Exactly one of the five approved purposes. Any other value fails the gate.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. turn_ids supporting this reference.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type is VERBATIM and carries a source_quote anchored by byte offset. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the gate enforces presence + byte-match for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Verbatim transcript substring supporting this item. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset of source_quote against the normalized transcript. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence and byte-match at this offset.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the original transcript (re-derived by the gate from the normalization position map).

reason.description: Phase 3.A (additive, optional). Why the speaker is making this claim/decision/action/etc. Must be derivable from the source span. Required by the prompt; optional in JSON Schema for backward-compat with pre-3.A artifacts.

source_chunk_id.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). Optional chunk identifier the source_quote was lifted from. When present, the Phase 4.A substring grounding gate (`promotion/grounding_gate.py`) scopes its check to this chunk only. When absent, the gate falls back to checking against the full transcript text (weaker — and logged as a warning in the grounding_gate_result artifact). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy 1.0.0-1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

external_stakeholder_input.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. Input relayed from parties NOT present in the meeting (industry associations, ITU, congressional offices, OSD). Only when a speaker explicitly relays it — a bare document mention with no relayed content is NOT stakeholder input. Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

input_type.description: Exactly one of the six approved input types. Any other value fails the gate.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. turn_ids where the input was relayed.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type is VERBATIM and carries a source_quote anchored by byte offset. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the gate enforces presence + byte-match for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Verbatim transcript substring supporting this item. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset of source_quote against the normalized transcript. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence and byte-match at this offset.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the original transcript (re-derived by the gate from the normalization position map).

reason.description: Phase 3.A (additive, optional). Why the speaker is making this claim/decision/action/etc. Must be derivable from the source span. Required by the prompt; optional in JSON Schema for backward-compat with pre-3.A artifacts.

source_chunk_id.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). Optional chunk identifier the source_quote was lifted from. When present, the Phase 4.A substring grounding gate (`promotion/grounding_gate.py`) scopes its check to this chunk only. When absent, the gate falls back to checking against the full transcript text (weaker — and logged as a warning in the grounding_gate_result artifact). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy 1.0.0-1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

glossary_definition.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. A term formally defined or clarified for the purpose of this study. At scale this builds a living domain glossary from the TIG process. Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

authoritative.description: Optional. Default false (applied by the producer/model, not enforced by JSON Schema); true only when the speaker indicated this is the official/working definition for the study.

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. turn_ids where the term was defined.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type is VERBATIM and carries a source_quote anchored by byte offset. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the gate enforces presence + byte-match for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Verbatim transcript substring supporting this item. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset of source_quote against the normalized transcript. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence and byte-match at this offset.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the original transcript (re-derived by the gate from the normalization position map).

procedural_ruling.description: Optional (schema_version 1.3.0+), default []. A chair / co-lead ruling on meeting procedure, scope boundaries, or process — DISTINCT from a `decisions` item (substantive). Establishes the governance framework. Legacy artifacts omit it (additivity).

ruling_type.description: Exactly one of the six approved ruling types. Any other value fails the gate.

binding.description: Optional. Default true (applied by the producer/model, not enforced by JSON Schema).

source_turns.description: Optional, default []. turn_ids supporting this ruling.

grounding_mode.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Discriminator: this item type is VERBATIM and carries a source_quote anchored by byte offset. Optional in schema (legacy artifacts omit it); the gate enforces presence + byte-match for 1.4.0 artifacts.

source_quote.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Verbatim transcript substring supporting this item. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence.

quote_offset_normalized.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset of source_quote against the normalized transcript. Optional in schema; the gate enforces presence and byte-match at this offset.

quote_offset_original.description: Phase 1 (schema_version 1.4.0). Byte offset against the original transcript (re-derived by the gate from the normalization position map).

reason.description: Phase 3.A (additive, optional). Why the speaker is making this claim/decision/action/etc. Must be derivable from the source span. Required by the prompt; optional in JSON Schema for backward-compat with pre-3.A artifacts.

source_chunk_id.description: Phase 4.A G-GROUND-VERBATIM (schema_version 1.5.0). Optional chunk identifier the source_quote was lifted from. When present, the Phase 4.A substring grounding gate (`promotion/grounding_gate.py`) scopes its check to this chunk only. When absent, the gate falls back to checking against the full transcript text (weaker — and logged as a warning in the grounding_gate_result artifact). Optional at the JSON-Schema layer so legacy 1.0.0-1.4.0 artifacts validate unchanged.

grounding.description: Phase Y (schema_version 1.1.0): one entry per extracted item with its source_turns. Shape left loose here so the additive change does not re-pin the Phase Y grounding contract.

provenance.description: Producer metadata. Does NOT set `additionalProperties: false` so phase-additive blocks (e.g. Phase 2's `extraction_config`) attach without bumping schema_version.

chunking_strategy_version.description: Phase 2.B (optional, additive). Identifies the chunking strategy used to produce this artifact. Values: `speaker_turn_v1` (current behaviour, zero overlap; default for any artifact omitting the field) or `speaker_turn_v1_overlap{N}` (overlap of N turns, e.g. `speaker_turn_v1_overlap2`). The comparison engine halts with `chunking_strategy_mismatch` when two artifacts under comparison declare incompatible values. Pre-Phase-2.B artifacts validate unchanged because the field is optional and `additionalProperties` on `provenance` is intentionally not strict (see this object's description).

extraction_config.description: Phase 2 (optional, additive). Captured by `pipeline.governed_pipeline_run` so the run can be re-executed from the artifact alone. Two identical-config invocations must produce identical comparison F1; a perturbed `prompt_content_hash` must produce a different F1. Absent on legacy (pre-Phase-2) artifacts; the comparison engine treats those as `legacy_eval: true`. Phase 3 adds three optional glossary metadata fields: `glossary_version_hash`, `glossary_tokens_added`, `tainted_glossary_drift`. The hash + tokens fields must be present together or absent together (enforced by `pipeline.governed_run.validate_glossary_metadata_consistency`, which JSON Schema cannot natively express because each field is optional in isolation).

glossary_version_hash.description: Phase 3 (optional). sha256 of the glossary file at load time. Present together with `glossary_tokens_added` (enforced by `validate_glossary_metadata_consistency`); absent when glossary injection was disabled. Legacy (pre-Phase-3) artifacts omit it entirely.

glossary_tokens_added.description: Phase 3 (optional). Total tokens added across all batches from glossary terminology blocks. Present together with `glossary_version_hash`; 0 is valid (injection was enabled but no batch matched). Absent when injection was disabled.

tainted_glossary_drift.description: Phase 3 (optional). True when the glossary file's sha256 changed between load and completion. A tainted run is excluded from per-source variance budget calculations (same lifecycle as `legacy_eval: true`). Omitted on runs that did not enable glossary injection.

prompt_variant.description: Phase 5 (optional). Identifies the (prompt, model) combination that produced this artifact. Optional for backward compat — pre-Phase-5 artifacts omit it; the comparison engine defaults a missing value to `production_haiku`. Two artifacts with the same `produced_by` model but different `prompt_variant` are NOT compared directly — they are shown side-by-side in the three-way table as separate columns. Phase 6 adds `production_haiku_with_cascade_filter`: stamped on the `meeting_minutes_filtered` artifact when Stage 2 cascade is enabled (the source `meeting_minutes` keeps `production_haiku`). The enum extension is additive: pre-Phase-6 artifacts validate unchanged.
